step_by_step_reasoning: First, the customer (UserConsumerMobile) initiates a call to Amazon Connect. Connect performs a data dip via a Lambda function that queries a CRM (ThirdParty) and leverages Lex for natural language processing. Once routed, the caller interacts with the Agent. After the call, Connect exports recordings to an S3 bucket, which triggers post-call processing using Transcribe and Comprehend. Simultaneously, Connect streams real-time interaction events via Kinesis and Firehose into a separate S3 analytics bucket, which is queried using Athena for reporting.

graph.name: Intuit Global Contact Center Architecture with Amazon Connect
graph.notes: Serverless contact center solution built using Amazon Connect. Scales dynamically to handle seasonal TurboTax tax filing peaks while providing real-time data dips and post-call analytics.

nodes.name: Taxpayer Customer
nodes.notes: End-user calling in for tax assistance.

nodes.name: Amazon Connect
nodes.notes: Core contact center service handling routing and telephony integration.

nodes.name: CRM Data Dip Lambda
nodes.notes: Retrieves caller profiles and routing context.

nodes.name: Amazon Lex
nodes.notes: Handles natural language interactions for routing.

nodes.name: Call Recordings S3
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores raw audio recordings from call sessions.

nodes.name: Amazon Transcribe
nodes.notes: Converts call audio to text transcriptions.

nodes.name: Amazon Comprehend
nodes.notes: Extracts sentiment and key topics from transcriptions.

nodes.name: Kinesis Data Firehose
nodes.notes: Buffers and loads event streams into S3.

nodes.name: Analytics S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: Stores partitioned reporting and event data.

nodes.name: Amazon Athena
nodes.notes: Enables interactive ad-hoc SQL queries on event logs.
